diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lightheadedness is common with hypocapnia and anxiety, but is non-specific.
Acute isolated dizziness in an older adult can be posterior-circulation ischaemia and cannot be excluded on history alone.
Anaemia can cause dizziness, but no measured Hb is available remotely.

symptoms.character: numbness and tingling of all fingers of both hands simultaneously

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral symmetrical distal (and peri-oral) paraesthesia is the classic hyperventilation-tetany pattern from respiratory alkalosis.
Bilateral symmetrical symptoms are atypical for a single-territory stroke, though a brainstem lesion can rarely be bilateral.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Subacute combined degeneration of the cord
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: SCD causes symmetrical distal sensory symptoms, but insidiously over weeks — the acute onset argues against it.
A subjective sense of profound weakness without objective power loss is common in panic; objective power cannot be tested remotely.
Weakness must be treated as possibly neurological until objective power is examined in person.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid breathing / acute panic during episode

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Observed rapid breathing with acute fear is the mechanism generating the tetany paraesthesia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of FAST features — no facial droop, no slurred speech, no true syncope

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of FAST features lowers the probability of anterior-circulation stroke but does NOT exclude posterior-circulation stroke.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known borderline-low B12 with mild macrocytosis (patient-reported prior test)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Subacute combined degeneration of the cord
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: B12 deficiency is the substrate for SCD, but early mild deficiency rarely causes acute neurological events.
The frightening diagnosis is the precipitant for the health anxiety and panic that drive the hyperventilation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal neurological examination — intact cranial nerves, 5/5 power, intact fine touch and proprioception, negative Romberg

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal neurological examination is what excludes an acute central lesion — clinician-only, sealed; the AI Doctor cannot obtain it and must escalate for it.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Subacute combined degeneration of the cord
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intact proprioception and a negative Romberg exclude posterior-column disease — not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: No postural blood-pressure drop on supervised testing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No orthostatic drop argues against significant hypovolaemia/anaemia — but supervised postural testing is clinician-measured, not a home reading.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without serology the autoimmune basis of the B12 deficiency is not established.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Subacute combined degeneration of the cord
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms autoimmune (pernicious) aetiology of the B12 deficiency — a laboratory result, sealed, not obtainable in real time via telehealth.

investigation_details.investigation_name: FBC with film and serum B12
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The degree of anaemia and B12 level cannot be quantified without bloods — telehealth cannot exclude symptomatic anaemia on history.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Only a measured Hb excludes haemodynamically significant anaemia — never available in real time in telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Bilateral symmetrical distal paraesthesia arising during observed rapid breathing and acute fear is the characteristic benign hyperventilation-tetany constellation — but is a diagnosis of exclusion in an older adult with acute neuro symptoms.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person neurological examination (cranial nerves, power, sensation, proprioception, Romberg, gait)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single most important next step — it is what distinguishes benign hyperventilation from stroke/TIA and SCD, and Breath-Ezy cannot perform it.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count with film and serum B12/folate
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies the anaemia and B12 status to plan replacement and exclude symptomatic anaemia.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent brain imaging (CT/MRI) via the assessing service
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: If any FAST feature, persistent focal deficit or diagnostic doubt emerges at in-person assessment, imaging is required to exclude stroke — ordered by the assessing clinician, not the AI Doctor.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Arterial blood gas / repeated home BP series for orthostasis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not an appropriate remote first-line step; an ABG is invasive and unnecessary, and self-directed repeated readings should not delay in-person assessment.
